title: K Sum

content.problemStatement:
Given an array arr of positive integers and an integer kkk, write a program that returns the maximum possible sum of kkk elements taken from either the start of the array, the end of the array, or a combination of both.


Input Format

The first line contains two positive integers nnn and kkk representing the size of arrarrarr and number of elements whose sum is to be taken.
Next line contains nnn elements of the array.


Output Format
Print the maximum sum that can be achieved by taking kkk elements.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers n and k (1 ≤ k ≤ n). The second line contains n positive integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the maximum possible sum of exactly k elements taken from the beginning of the array, the end of the array, or a combination of both.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Take 1 element from the start (4) and 2 elements from the end (5 and 1). Sum = 4+5+1 = 10.
k equals n, so we must take all elements. Sum = 1+2+3 = 6.
With k = 1 we can only pick the first or the last element. The last element (5) is larger.

solution.approach: The optimal choice is to take some prefix of length i (0 ≤ i ≤ k) from the start and the remaining k‑i elements from the end. By scanning all possible i we can compute the sum in O(k) time. We first compute the sum of the first k elements (i=k). Then we slide the window: each step removes the next element from the left side of the prefix and adds the next element from the right side of the suffix. The maximum encountered sum is the answer. This runs in O(k) per test case, which is O(n) in the worst case, and uses O(1) extra space.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{FastScanner fs=new FastScanner(System.in);int T=fs.nextInt();StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();while(T-- >0){int n=fs.nextInt();int k=fs.nextInt();long[] a=new long[n];for(int i=0;i<n;i++)a[i]=fs.nextLong();long[] pref=new long[k+1];pref[0]=0;for(int i=1;i<=k;i++)pref[i]=pref[i-1]+a[i-1];long[] suff=new long[k+1];suff[0]=0;for(int i=1;i<=k;i++)suff[i]=suff[i-1]+a[n-i];long best=Long.MIN_VALUE;for(int i=0;i<=k;i++){long cur=pref[i]+suff[k-i];if(cur>best)best=cur;}sb.append(best).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}static class FastScanner{private final BufferedInputStream in;private final byte[] buffer=new byte[1<<16];private int ptr=0,len=0;FastScanner(InputStream stream){in=new BufferedInputStream(stream);}private int readByte() throws IOException{if(ptr>=len){len=in.read(buffer);ptr=0;if(len<=0)return-1;}return buffer[ptr++];}long nextLong() throws IOException{int c;while((c=readByte())!=-1&&c<=' ');boolean neg=false;if(c=='-'){neg=true;c=readByte();}long val=0;while(c>' '){val=val*10+(c-'0');c=readByte();}return neg?-val:val;}int nextInt() throws IOException{return (int)nextLong();}}}
